title: Monopsony: Foundational concepts
description: Practice what you've learned about the foundational concepts about monopsony markets in this exercise.

assessment_items.question_content:
**A monopsony market is one in which:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: there is one buyer and many sellers.
there are many buyers and many sellers.
there are a few buyers and a few sellers.
there is one seller and many buyers.
there are a few sellers and many buyers.

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider two characteristics of a monopsony labor market: the number of firms hiring and firms’ hiring incentives. 

**Which of the following correctly describe these two characteristics?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a single firm hiring labor that must adjust the wage it pays
a single firm hiring labor that can hire as much labor as it wants at the market wage
one of many firms hiring labor that can hire as much labor as it wants at the market wage
one of many firms hiring labor whose marginal factor cost of labor is the same for all units of labor
a single firm hiring labor whose marginal factor cost of labor is less than the supply of labor

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes a *monopsony* market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: one buyer, many sellers
many buyers, one seller
many buyers, many sellers
a few buyers, many sellers
many buyers, a few sellers

assessment_items.question_content:
A single firm in Aluminum Valley hires computer coders. The market supply of computer coder labor is given here.

Wage (per hour) | Quantity of labor supplied | Total cost of labor
-|-|-
$\$10$ | $1$ | $\$10$
$\$20$ | $2$ | $\$40$
$\$30$ | $3$ | $\$90$
$\$40$ | $4$ | $\$160$
$\$50$ | $5$ | $\$250$


**What is the marginal factor cost of the fifth worker?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

A single college in a small town hires instructors. The market supply of college instructors is given here.

Salary(per year) | Quantity of labor supplied | Total cost of labor
-|-|-
$\$50{,}000$ | $1$ | $\$50{,}000$
$\$60{,}000$ | $2$ | $\$120{,}000$
$\$70{,}00$ | $3$ | $\$210{,}000$
$\$80{,}000$ | $4$ | $\$320{,}000$
$\$90{,}000$ | $5$ | $\$450{,}000$


**What is the marginal factor cost of the second instructor?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Hogwarts is the only firm hiring teachers. The labor supply of wizardry teachers is given in the market shown here.

Wage (per day) | Quantity of labor supplied 
-|-
$\$300$ | $0$
$\$400$ | $1$
$\$500$ | $2$
$\$600$ | $3$
$\$700$ | $4$

**What is the marginal factor cost of the second unit of labor?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To find the marginal factor cost, you must first find the total cost of hiring $2$ units of labor.

The total cost of hiring $2$ units of labor is $\$1000$:

$\begin{align} \text{total cost of labor} &= \text{quantity of labor hired} \times \text{wage}\\\\
&=2 \times \$500\\\\
&=\$1000\end{align}$

To find the marginal cost of the second unit of labor, subtract the total cost of $1$ unit from the total cost of $2$ units. $1$ unit of labor costs $\$400$.

The marginal cost of the second unit of labor is $\$600$:

$\$1000 - \$400 = \$600$

assessment_items.question_content:
**A firm that hires labor in a monopsonistic market hires a quantity of labor which is**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: less than in a perfectly competitive market, and pays a wage lower than a perfectly competitive firm
greater than in a perfectly competitive market, and pays the same wage as would exist in a perfectly competitive market.
less than in a perfectly competitive market, and pays the same wage as would exist in a perfectly competitive market.
less than in a perfectly competitive market, and the marginal factor cost of that quantity is lower than the wage rate.
greater than in a perfectly competitive market, and the marginal factor cost of that quantity is higher than the wage rate.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements is true about a monopsony labor market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The marginal factor cost of labor is greater than the wage rate
The firm can set wages at whatever price they want.
The profit maximizing choice of labor will be at the intersection of labor supply and labor demand.
There is one seller and many buyers.
There are no profits.

assessment_items.question_content:
A single large hospital system hires all nurses in a city. Assume that this is a for-profit hospital system.

**Which of the following can we predict about this market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Nurses are paid less than they would be paid in a competitive labor market.
More nurses will be hired than in a competitive labor market.
The marginal factor cost of labor is less than the supply of labor.
The hospital system will hire the quantity of labor where the supply of labor equals the marginal revenue product of labor.
The quantity of labor hired will be allocatively efficient.

assessment_items.question_content:
The market for factory labor in a town had been competitive, but All of the factories got together and merged into a single firm. As a result, there is now a single firm hiring labor in this market.

**Which of the following can we predict about this market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The firm will hire fewer workers and pay a lower wage.
The firm will hire more workers but wages are unaffected.
The marginal factor cost of labor is less than the supply of labor.
The firm will hire more workers and pay a lower wage.
The firm will hire fewer workers but wages are unaffected.

assessment_items.question_content:
**If a labor market changes from being competitive to a monopsony, what will happen?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Employment decreases and wages decrease.
Employment increases and wages are unaffected.
Employment increases but wages decrease.
Employment decreases but wages are unaffected.
Employment increases and wages increase.

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph shown here illustrates the labor market in a small town.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4bc4f7b866855339edc8d40fbdc1205b7f9f8d47)

**What is the competitive market wage and what is the monopsony wage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Competitive market wage: $w_d$, Monopsony wage: $w_e$
Competitive market wage: $w_c$, Monopsony wage: $w_e$
Competitive market wage: $w_d$, Monopsony wage: $w_b$
Competitive market wage: $w_d$, Monopsony wage: $w_c$
Competitive market wage: $w_c$, Monopsony wage: $w_b$

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph shown here illustrates the labor market in place with a single employer.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6651e41e430321c979956cb9de05e1b8fe928409)

**What is the quantity of labor a monopsony firm would hire, and what is the wage it will pay?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The graph shown here illustrates the labor market in place with a single employer.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6651e41e430321c979956cb9de05e1b8fe928409)

**What is the competitive wage in this market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: $w_t$
$w_u$
$w_x$
$w_y$
$w_z$

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph shown here illustrates the labor market in place with a single employer.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fa79da35945e546ae22f701f8f7c04ee640b9b5e)

**What wage will a *monopsony* in this market pay?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
